mnemonics.title: Test Plan Components
mnemonics.content: Use acronym SPeDSRS: Scope, Plan, deliverables, Schedule, Resources, Risk, Strategy - helps remember major Test Plan elements

mnemonics.title: Test Levels Remember
mnemonics.content: Unit, Integration, System, Acceptance - Remember: UISA (You Integrate System Acceptably)

mnemonics.title: Testing Techniques
mnemonics.content: Black Box: Equivalence, Boundary, Decision Table, State Transition - Remember: EEBS (Equivalence, Equivalence, Boundary, State)

acronyms.usage: Phases of testing from planning to closure
Maps requirements to test cases for complete coverage
Process of ensuring software quality through planned activities
Final testing by end users to accept the system

visualTips.visualization: Think of Test Plan as a tree - trunk is strategy, branches are schedule/deliverables, leaves are test cases, roots are entry/exit criteria
Visualize a pie chart showing percentage of requirements covered by test cases - aim for 100% coverage
Use a gate metaphor - Entry gate opens when preconditions met, Exit gate opens when completion criteria satisfied

analogies.analogy: Like an architectural blueprint before building a house - guides all construction activities
Like the general's battle plan - overall approach before specific tactics
Like a movie set - must replicate real-world settings for authentic filming
Like a table of contents in a book - tracks where each requirement is covered
Like a car's dashboard - provides quantitative feedback on testing health
